get.summary: Gets files previously assigned to the account.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the AssetAccount.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Get past files that were active after this date. Defaults to 1 day before endDate. (Preferred over 'filter').

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Get past files that were active before this date. Defaults to now. (Preferred over filter).

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter results. Available operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, contains, ieq, icontains, sw, isw, ew, iew, in [ {item1}, {item2}, etc], (). Use \ to escape quotes, asterisks and backslashes in strings.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Which page (starting with 0) of data to return.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The size of a page of data.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When specified, the method will return a single integer value representing the total number of items that match the given filter criteria. If specified, the fields and orderby parameter values are ignored.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to include in entity output. Prepend list with '-' to exclude from output.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to sort entities by. Prepend properties with '-' for descending.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search all string fields for the specified value.

            - Ssh2 - Tectia format for use with tools from SSH.com

            - Putty - Putty format for use with PuTTY tools.

post.summary:
When set to `true.` on a provider, the login page will not display a drop down list
            of all available providers. Instead, the end user will be defaulted in to using the specified provider.
            Only one provider can be marked as the default at a time. When updating the specified provider,
            any previously set default will be cleared.
            
If a default provider is set and you need to log in using some other provider, like the
            Safeguard Local provider in order to log in as a local administrator user, a query string parameter
            can be appended to the login page URL, 'primaryProviderID', where the value is set to the 'RstsProviderId'
            you need. For example, "https://<safeguard>/RSTS/Login?response_type=token&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2F<safeguard>%2F&primaryProviderID=local".
You cannot set a provider that is used for two-factor authentication as the default.
This functionality is only applicable to web browser based logins, not programmatic API/OAuth2 logins.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique ID of the AuthenticationProvider.

400.description: 60941 - The specified provider type, can only be used for two-factor authentication. Only primary authentication providers can be marked as the default.

parameters.description: Unique ID of a Directory IdentityProvider.

parameters.name: searchBase
parameters.description:
Sets the searchBase for the Ldap query, defaults to base of the domain for ldap, or base of
forest for AD.  Must be in DN Syntax.

parameters.name: searchScope
parameters.description: Defines the scope of the query, either base, one, or sub, defaults to sub.

parameters.name: searchName
parameters.description: Sets a search constraint on the "name" of the object to return.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter results. Available operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, contains, ieq, icontains, sw, isw, ew, iew, in [ {item1}, {item2}, etc], (). Use \ to escape quotes, asterisks and backslashes in strings.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Which page (starting with 0) of data to return.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The size of a page of data.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: When specified, the method will return a single integer value representing the total number of items that match the given filter criteria. If specified, the fields and orderby parameter values are ignored.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to include in entity output. Prepend list with '-' to exclude from output.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to sort entities by. Prepend properties with '-' for descending.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search all string fields for the specified value.

403.description: Must have UserAdmin, SystemAuditor or Auditor permission.

post.summary:
If an external federation provider has been configured with an HTTPS URL for the metadata, Safeguard
            automatically monitors it and will periodically download the metadata to check for updates. In certain scenarios,
            you may need to immediately trigger an update. For example, when changing the signing certificates on an
            external STS that doesn't support assigning multiple active certificates at once for seamless rollover.


            This method can be invoked from any node in a cluster, if needed. An attempt will then be made to also update
            all other nodes in the cluster.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the IdentityProvider.

400.description:
61007 - The specified identity provider must have a TypeReferenceName of ExternalFederation.
            
61008 - The specified identity provider must have the FederationMetadata property set to an HTTPS URL.
            
61005 - An unknown error occurred while attempting to refresh the external federation provider's metadata. Please see the logs for more details.
            
61006 - An error occurred while attempting to refresh the external federation provider's metadata. Additional information will be included.

get.summary: Gets the entitlements for the current user by account.

parameters.name: accessRequestType
parameters.description: Only report on access via a specific request type.

parameters.name: assetIds
parameters.description:
List of asset IDs to get entitlements for (preferred over filter). Specify a
            comma delimited list to return all accessible accounts for each specified asset ID. If you also specify
            accountIds, the corresponding set of entitlements between all IDs will be returned.
            If a value is specified here, it will take precedence over the assetName.

parameters.name: accountIds
parameters.description:
List of account IDs to get entitlements for (preferred over filter). Specify a
            comma delimited list to return each accessible account. If you also specify assetIds,
            the corresponding set of entitlements between all IDs will be returned. If a value is specified here,
            it will take precedence over the accountName.

parameters.name: assetName
parameters.description:
Search text for asset name (preferred over filter). Performs a 'contains' style search.
            The cacheCookie parameter should be unique per search value specified.

parameters.name: accountName
parameters.description:
Search text for account name (preferred over filter). Performs a 'contains' style search.
            The cacheCookie parameter should be unique per search value specified.

parameters.name: includeActiveRequests
parameters.description: Whether to include information about active requests for same account.

parameters.name: filterByCredential
parameters.description: Whether to filter out entitlements and policies that don't have valid credential configurations.

parameters.name: cacheCookie
parameters.description:
A client generated value, such that if specified, the results will be cached on the server
            and subsequent calls by the client with the same cookie value will return the same cached results, as permitted by
            the memory and other activity on the server. For example, a client can pass in a date time stamp, or random string.
            Then, while paging or searching the data, make subsequent calls with the same value. If the end user needs to refresh,
            they should be able to navigate away and come back and a new cookie value be generated.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter results. Available operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, contains, ieq, icontains, sw, isw, ew, iew, in [ {item1}, {item2}, etc], (). Use \ to escape quotes, asterisks and backslashes in strings.

get.summary: Gets all of the application's settings.
get.description:
Setting Name Description
Inform User of Bad Password During login, using the Local provider, display a friendly error message if the user's password is incorrect.
Inform User of Disabled Account During login, using the Local provider, display a friendly error message if the user's account is disabled.
Inform User of Locked Account During login, using the Local provider, display a friendly error message if the user's account is locked.
Inform User of Unknown User During login, using the Local provider, display a friendly error message if the specified username doesn't exist.
Inform User of Invalid Token Return a friendly error message and reason when an invalid or expired authentication token is used when making an API request. Otherwise, a generic "Access denied" error will be returned.
User Lockout Duration The amount of time, in minutes, when using the Local provider for which a locked user will not be able to log in.
User Lockout Threshold The number of consecutive failed log in attempts before the user is marked as locked.
User Lockout Window The amount of time, in minutes, when using the Local provider for which the User Lockout Threshold will be accumulated.
Disable After Inactive for n Days The amount of time, in days, when using the Local provider after which a user will be marked as disabled and cannot log in.
Maximum Password Age The amount of time, in days, when using the Local provider after which the user must change their password.
Minimum Password Age The amount of time, in days, when using the Local provider for which a user cannot change their password.
Password History When using the Local provider, the number of unique new passwords that must be used when a user changes their password before an old password can be reused.
Password Grace Period During login, using the Local provider, display a password expiration reminder this many days before a user's password will expire.
Token Lifetime The amount of time, in seconds, for which an API authentication token is valid for. This also corresponds to the amount of time you can remain logged into the Safeguard Web UI before being logged out.
Inform User of Expired Password During login, using the Local provider, display a friendly error message if the user's password is expired.
Maximum Notification Recipients Each set of approvers, reviewers, and emergency contacts can have up to this many recipients.
Expiration Warning Duration Days The number of days before an Access Request Policy is set to expire at which a PolicyAdmin will receive a reminder.
Default User Timezone Available time zones that can be assigned to users as their default time zone.
Max Platform Task Retries The number of retries that will be attempted on a platform task, such as Change Password.
Web Client Inactivity Timeout The amount of idle time, in minutes, after which a user will be automatically logged out of the Safeguard web site.
Use Fine Grained UserGroup Permissions Use fine grained UserGroup permissions, allowing more Admins to perform CRUD operations on UserGroups. Set to false to revert to previous behavior.
Show Friendly Authentication Error Messages When set to true, users authenticating against Active Directory or LDAP will have friendly, specific error messages displayed when the login attempt fails. This includes individual messages for "Invalid password" or "Unknown username", as well as other possibilities, depending on the provider, like "The specified account has been locked". When set to false, only a generic error message of "Invalid username or bad password" will ever be displayed on the login page.
Show Access Request Conflict User Name Show the conflicting user name in the access request error message if true else show the access request id. Showing the user name could be considered a security risk.
Allow Access Request Search By Tags Allow requesters to search for Assets and Accounts using tag names and tag descriptions.
Allowed OAuth2 Grant Types Specify what authorization grants, or flows, the server will allow a client application to use. The current recommendation is to only allow the authorization code grant type with PKCE, which is the default used by Safeguard itself and cannot be disabled. It is recommended to disable all other grant types unless you have a custom integration application using them.
Trusted Servers Restrict login redirects and CORS requests to the specified list of IP addresses, host names (including DNS wildcards), and CIDR notation networks. By default, a single asterisk means there are no restrictions. You should change or delete this value after configuring your cluster. Set to the empty string to prevent any external access.
Filter Access Request Accounts By Credential Filters out assets and accounts that don't have a credential from the list a requester can request. There are performance implications when this is enabled.
Cache Access Request Accounts Cache the access request accounts per requester. This results in increased memory usage and potentially stale data, but it can provide a performance improvement for large data sets.
Secure Files Disk Usage Threshold MB Specify the amount of used disk space at which the appliance will restrict uploading files as account secrets.
Secure File Max File Size MB Specify the max size of file that may be uploaded as an account.
Preload New Access Request List Within the Safeguard for Privileged Passwords web UI, when a user navigates to Access Requests -> My Requests and clicks the New Request button, the page will initially show a table loaded with all of the user's accessible accounts. This may cause additional, unnecessary load on the server. If this setting is turned off, the page will not initially load the user's accounts, and instead require the user to first enter an asset and/or account name to search for.
Administered Certificate Notify Days Before Expire Number of days before an administered certificate expires to notify the owners.
Administered Certificate Notify Days After Expire Number of days after an administered certificate expires that the owners will continue to be notified.

get.summary:
Controls the 'ForceAuthn' attribute of a SAML2 AuthnRequest. When set to true, the user will
be required to reenter their credentials on the external federation site, even if they are already
logged in, thus negating any single sign-on benefits, but may be considered more secure.
Deprecated: Use /v4/Starling/Subscription instead.

204.description:
Must be joined to Starling and have the 'StarlingDirectory' Identity Provider be created
            by the system to get or set this property.

403.description: Must have GlobalAdmin, UserAdmin, HelpdeskAdmin, ApplianceAdmin, OperationsAdmin, PolicyAdmin, AssetAdmin, PartitionAdmin, ApplicationAuditor, SystemAuditor or Auditor permission.

put.summary:
Controls the 'ForceAuthn' attribute of a SAML2 AuthnRequest. When set to true, the user will
be required to reenter their credentials on the external federation site, even if they are already
logged in, thus negating any single sign-on benefits, but may be considered more secure.
Deprecated: Use /v4/Starling/Subscription instead.

requestBody.description:
Set to true to require the user to reenter their credentials on
            the external federation site, even if they are already logged in. Set to false to allow the user to
            automatically be logged into Safeguard if the external federation site supports SSO.

400.description:
60459 - Must be joined to Starling to get or set this property.
            
60988 - The Starling Identity Provider does not exist. Please ensure the appliance has Internet access.
            You may need to wait up to three hours for the system to create the Identity Provider.

403.description: Must have GlobalAdmin or ApplianceAdmin permission.

post.summary:
After obtaining an access token from an STS, a client application must exchange that token for a Safeguard user
            token that can then be used to access all API methods.  This method will attempt to authorize the user from the STS and
            if successful, will return a new token value.

requestBody.description:
Currently, just the `access_token.` from the OAuth2 protocol is needed.  In the future, other
            properties may be added.

200.description:
The client application should check the value of the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.LoginResponse.Status
            property to determine the next course of action.

post.summary:
An explicit logout by an end user to have their Safeguard User Token deleted from the system such that it
            cannot be used again.
post.description:
Call this method like any other API method that requires authorization.  Your token must currently be valid.
            If your token has already expired or previously been deleted, an Http 401 response will be returned.

parameters.name: timedOut
parameters.description: A value indicating whether the logout was due to inactivity or not. Defaults to false.

204.description: The user's current Safeguard User Token has successfully been deleted from the system.

401.description:
You must call this method with a valid, non-expired authorization token.  If the token has previously
            been deleted, or it has already expired, then you will get the standard
            `{"Code":60094,"Message":"Authorization is denied for this request.","InnerError":null}` in an Http 401 response.

post.summary:
Directory Groups: If you ever change the primary or secondary authentication providers of a directory based User Group, those changes
will not be reflected on existing users within Safeguard.  A user that was added to Safeguard via a directory group will only
ever have their authentication providers set when the user object is first created in Safeguard.  Subsequent background
synchronizations and changes to the User Group won't effect those existing user's authentication provider settings.  If,
however, you do need to force the authentication providers to be updated on existing users, then you must call this method.
Local Groups:  Call this to simply synchronize a local group with its members to ensure AdminRoles on the members are correct.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique ID of UserGroup.

202.description: Success - group membership will be updated in the background.

AllowSimultaneousAccess.description: Whether to allow more than one access request to be active at the same time.

MaximumSimultaneousReleases.description: Maximum number of times access can be granted during the same time period.

ChangePasswordAfterCheckin.description: Whether to change the account password after an access request is checked in.

ChangeSshKeyAfterCheckin.description: Whether to change the SSH key pair after an access request is checked in.

AllowSessionPasswordRelease.description: Whether to allow the password to be released as part of a session access requests. Deprecated: Use IncludePasswordRelease instead.

AllowSessionSshKeyRelease.description: Whether to allow the SSH key to be released as part of a session access request (Ssh). Deprecated: Use IncludeSshKeyRelease instead.

IncludePasswordRelease.description:
For use with session access request policies and access request policies of type Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.File.
When set to `true`, if the account for which the access request also contains a password, then that password will also be included as part of the access request.
E.g. making it immediately available for checkout along with a file request. This can be useful in cases where the file may be an encrypted Zip file, for example. That way, the user
doesn't have to make a separate access request just for the password.

If the account does not allow explicit Password Requests, but does allow File Requests, and this property is set to `true`, then the password will still be released.

IncludeSshKeyRelease.description:
For use with access request policies of type Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.File and Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.Ssh.
When set to `true`, if the account for which the access request also contains an SSH key, then that SSH key will also be included as part of the access request.
E.g. making it immediately available for checkout along with the file. That way, the user doesn't have to make a separate access request just for the SSH key.

If the account does not allow explicit SSH Key Requests, but does allow File Requests, and this property is set to `true`, then the SSH key will still be released.

RepeatInterval.description:
How frequently to run. Units are based on ScheduleType.
e.g. every 2 days, every 3 months, every 4 weeks.

RepeatDayOfMonth.description: Which day of the month the schedule should run. Only valid when RepeatMonthlyScheduleType is DayOfMonth.

RepeatDaysOfWeek.description: Which days of the week the schedule should run. Only valid when ScheduleType is Weekly.

StartMinute.description: The minute of the hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Minute' schedule type).

TimeOfDayIntervals.description: Configuration of intervals of time in a day where tasks can be executed.

MaxCharacters.description: The maximum length of the password.

MinCharacters.description: The minimum length of the password.

AllowUppercaseCharacters.description: Allow uppercase characters in the password?.

MinUppercaseCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the minimum number of uppercase characters required.

InvalidUppercaseCharacters.description: List of uppercase alphabet characters that may not be used.

MaxConsecutiveUppercaseCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the maximum number of uppercase characters that may be used consecutively.

AllowLowercaseCharacters.description: Allow lowercase characters in the password?.

MinLowercaseCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the minimum number of lowercase characters required.

InvalidLowercaseCharacters.description: List of lowercase alphabet characters that may not be used.

MaxConsecutiveLowercaseCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the maximum number of lowercase characters that may be used consecutively.

AllowNumericCharacters.description: Allow numeric characters in the password?.

MinNumericCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the minimum number of numeric characters required.

InvalidNumericCharacters.description: List of numeric characters that may not be used.

MaxConsecutiveNumericCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the maximum number of numeric characters that may be used consecutively.

AllowNonAlphaNumericCharacters.description: Allow non-alphanumeric characters in the password?.

MinNonAlphaNumericCharacters.description: If allowed, the minimum number of non-alphanumeric characters required.

AllowedNonAlphaNumericCharacters.description: List of non-alphanumeric characters that may be used when NonAlphaNumericRestrictionType is set to 'Include'.

InvalidNonAlphaNumericCharacters.description:
List of numeric characters that may not be used when NonAlphaNumericRestrictionType is set to 'Exclude'
NOTE: Generated passwords will be restricted to the set of printable ascii characters.

MaxConsecutiveNonAlphaNumericCharacters.description: If allowed, what is the maximum number of non-alphanumeric characters that may be used consecutively.

MaxConsecutiveAlphabeticCharacters.description: If upper or lower characters are allowed, what is the maximum number of alphabet characters that may be used consecutively.

MaxConsecutiveAlphaNumericCharacters.description: If upper or lower or numeric characters are allowed, what is the maximum number of alphabet or numeric characters that may be used consecutively.

AllowPasswordRequest.description:
When set to `true`, an end user may submit a new Password Access Request for the account.
When set to `false`, an end user will receive an error when trying to submit a new Password Access Request.

AllowSessionRequest.description:
When set to `true`, an end user may submit a new session based Access Request for the account.
When set to `false`, an end user will receive an error when trying to submit a new session based Access Request.

AllowSshKeyRequest.description:
When set to `true`, an end user may submit a new SSH Key Access Request for the account.
When set to `false`, an end user will receive an error when trying to submit a new SSH Key Access Request.

AllowApiKeyRequest.description:
When set to `true`, an end user may submit a new API Key Access Request for the account.
When set to `false`, an end user will receive an error when trying to submit a new API Key Access Request.

AllowFileRequest.description:
When set to `true`, an end user may submit a new file Access Request for the account.
When set to `false`, an end user will receive an error when trying to submit a new file Access Request.

AccountRequestType.description: Type of request that can be made using an account.

NOTE: Sync group takes precedence over profile setting above. Profile will be explicitly set.

ID of the SSH key sync group to assign this account to. (Read-only)
NOTE: SSH key Sync group takes precedence over SSH key profile setting above. Profile will be explicitly set.
NOTE: SSH key Sync priority will be set to 0.

ForceAsDefault.description:
When set to `true` on a provider, the login page will not display a drop down list
            of all available providers. Instead, the end user will be defaulted in to using this provider.
            Only one provider can be marked with `true` at a time. When updating a provider and setting
            this to `true`, this will be automatically set to `false` on all other providers.

AuthnContextComparison.description:
From Assertions and Protocols for the OASIS Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) V2.0 – Errata Composite
            section 3.3.2.2.1. Specifies the comparison method used to evaluate the requested context
            classes or statements, one of "exact", "minimum", "maximum", or "better".

BackgroundAttachment.description: Sets whether a background image scrolls with the rest of the page, or is fixed.

How frequently data imported from this directory should be synchronized with changes made in AD (in minutes).
Note, this value has no effect when used with Azure AD and will always be set to zero.

UseSslEncryption.description:
Whether to encrypt the connection with SSL. For an LDAP Identity Provider, if this value is not specified,
it will be defaulted to true. For an Active Directory Identity Provider, if this value is not specified,
it will be defaulted to false.

VerifySslCertificate.description:
Verify SSL certificate if using SSL. If SSL is being used, then this property will automatically be
overridden and set to true. The environment must have valid PKI certificates configured and Safeguard
must be able to trust and validate the server's SSL certificate.

EffectiveServiceAccountName.description: The name of the account for connecting to the asset whether the account is managed or not (Read-only).

The domain name of the service account if the task uses a Directory
or DirectoryAccount (Read-only).

DirectoryPlatformId.description: Platform ID of the directory this job belongs to (Read-only).

DirectoryPlatformDisplayName.description: Platform display name of the directory this job belongs to (Read-only).

DirectoryDiscoveryProperties.description: Properties used when using Directory DiscoveryType.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderId.description:
Specify the primary authentication provider that all members will use to authenticate when accessing
            Safeguard.  Note, this will only be applied to new users being added to Safeguard at the time the group is saved.
            That is, if a member of a directory group already has a corresponding Safeguard user, then that user's
            authentication settings will not change.  This behavior can be overridden by calling the /UserGroups/{id}/SynchronizeDirectoryGroupAndUpdateAuthenticationProvidersOnExistingUsers
            API method.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The friendly display name of the specified primary authentication provider.  (Read-only).

RequireCertificateAuthentication.description:
If set to `true`, then the user must authenticate with a client certificate. If they attempt to login
            with a username and password, for example, if they are using their Active Directory account, access will be denied.
            Cannot be disabled if enforced by Active Directory.  This is only applicable when Active Directory is being used
            to authenticate a user.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderId.description:
Specify the secondary authentication provider that all members will use when authenticating against
            Safeguard.  Note, this will only be applied to new users being added to Safeguard at the time the group is saved.
            That is, if a member of a directory group already has a corresponding Safeguard user, then that user's
            authentication settings will not change.  This behavior can be overridden by calling the /UserGroups/{id}/SynchronizeDirectoryGroupAndUpdateAuthenticationProvidersOnExistingUsers
            API method.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The friendly display name of the specified secondary authentication provider.  (Read-only).

LinkDirectoryAccounts.description:
When set to `true`, if the directory user has any managed objects in the directory that also exist
            as Directory Accounts in Safeguard, then those Directory Accounts in Safeguard will automatically be assigned as
            a Linked Account to the user in Safeguard.  Safeguard will look at the directory user's attribute specified in the
            schema attributes, for example, 'managedObjects' in Active Directory by default, to get the list of possible accounts.
            This includes flattening any directory security groups or organizational units.

AllowPersonalAccounts.description:
When set to `true`, will let the directory user create personal accounts
            in Safeguard.

AdminRoles.description:
Administrative roles that each imported user of the group will be granted. e.g. GlobalAdmin, DirectoryAdmin,
            Auditor, AssetAdmin, ApplianceAdmin, PolicyAdmin, UserAdmin, HelpdeskAdmin, OperationsAdmin.

DirectoryGroupSyncProperties.description:
When a User Group is imported into Safeguard from a directory such as Active Directory or OpenLDAP, you can
            configure certain properties of the imported users to be set based upon the value(s) of specified directory attributes
            of that user.  For example, you can set the Safeguard user's primary authentication provider to be something other
            than the directory itself, and then set the Safeguard user's primary authentication login name to the value of the
            'employeeID' attribute in the directory.

DirectoryId.description: The directory this object was imported from. Required if this is an object to import from a directory.

DirectoryName.description: The name of the directory this object was imported from. (Read-only).

DomainName.description: Name of the domain this object is from. Required if this is an object to import from a directory.

DistinguishedName.description: The distinguished name of the object (Read-only).

NameFilter.description: Filters accounts based on the name of the account (an ANR search is performed for Active Directory). Names can be specified as a regular expression.

GroupFilter.description: Filter accounts based on the names of groups in the directory the account belongs to. Names can be specified as a regular expression.

UidFilter.description: Filter accounts based on their uidNumber attribute (Active Directory only). IDs can be specified as a single ID or a range, e.g. 1 or 3-5.

RidFilter.description: Filter accounts based on their Windows relative account ID (last part of objectSid attribute) (Active Directory only). IDs can be specified as a single ID or a range, e.g. 1 or 3-5.

GidFilter.description: Filter accounts based on their gidNumber attribute (Active Directory only). IDs can be specified as a single ID or a range, e.g. 1 or 3-5.

PrimaryGidFilter.description: Filter accounts based on their primaryGroupID attribute (Active Directory only). IDs can be specified as a single ID or a range, e.g. 1 or 3-5.

DateLastAuthenticated.description: Updated every time the authenticator is used when logging in.

Fido2Authenticator.description:
Details about an individual FIDO2 authenticator (or 'token' or 'security key') that has been registered with our
            system.  A user can, and should, have at least two authenticators registered and associated with their account at all times
            in case they ever loose or break one, they will have a backup.

The Identity is either a group or a user.

IsDirectory.description: If this provider represents a directory. (Read-only).

RstsProviderScope.description: The RSTS provider scope, if this provider supports authentication. This value is for information only and is not normally needed. (Read-only).

IdentityProvider.description:
Represents an identity provider. Almost all identity providers are also implicitly authentication providers (depending on
the IdentityProviderType: AllowPrimaryAuthFlag and AllowSecondaryAuthFlag values).

Realm.description:
The DNS suffix name(s) for which the Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.IdentityProviderConfigExternalFederationProperties.FederationMetadata is applicable to.  This value needs to match
            the email address suffix of a user that intends to be authenticated. If the federated authentication supports more than
            one realm, enter each realm separated by a space, comma, or new line.

FederationMetadata.description:
The complete federation metadata Xml of the Identity Provider STS that a user will be redirected to for
            authentication. Or the HTTPS Url to the publicly available federation metadata of the Identity Provider STS.

AuthnContextClasses.description:
The `RequestedAuthnContext` in a SAML2 request, asking the IdP to authenticate the user with
            a specified authentication mechanism. For example, `urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PasswordProtectedTransport`.
            If specified, use one or more of the allowed context class URIs from the saml-authn-context-2.0-os.pdf
            (https://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/v2.0/saml-authn-context-2.0-os.pdf), section 3.4 Schemas.
            Specify multiple values by separating the strings with a space or comma.

NameIDFormat.description:
The `Format` attribute of the `NameID` element within the `Issuer` element of a
            SAML2 AuthnRequest. For example, `urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:entity`. If specified,
            use one of the allowed name identifier format identifiers as listed in section 8.3 of Assertions and
            Protocols for the OASIS Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) V2.0 – Errata Composite.

RequireAuthentication.description:
Controls the `ForceAuthn` attribute of a SAML2 AuthnRequest. When set to true, the user
             will be required to reenter their credentials on the external federation site, even if they are already
             logged in, thus negating any single sign-on benefits, but may be considered more secure.
            
             If not specified/included in the request when creating a new instance, the default value will be true.

ApplicationIdOverride.description: Override the `Issuer` name value sent in a SAML2 AuthnRequest.

IdentityProviderConfigExternalFederation.description:
When the TypeReferenceName property is "ExternalFederation", this provides additional configuration values
            specific to that type.

DomainSuffix.description:
A valid domain string that identifies the WebAuthn Relying Party on whose behalf a given registration
            or authentication ceremony is being performed.  A public key credential can only be used for authentication
            with the same entity (as identified by this value) it was registered with.  However, this value may be just a
            valid registrable domain suffix of what appears in the user's browser when registering.  For example, you may
            enter just "contoso.com" here, which would allow you to register against a server at https://www.contoso.com,
            or https://node1.contoso.com, and later use the same authenticator security key to authenticate at either of
            those locations.

IdentityProviderConfigFido2Properties.description:
When the TypeReferenceName property is "Fido2", this provides additional FIDO2-specific
            configuration values.

DnsHostName.description:
The full DNS host name of the OneLogin subscription. Typically this will be <subdomain>.onelogin.com.
Where <subdomain> is the name chosen by you when first signing up.

ClientId.description:
The Client ID of your OneLogin API Credential pair. Obtained by creating API Credentials on your
OneLogin administration site. The credential must be given the "Manage All" scope/permission.

ClientSecret.description:
The Client Secret of your OneLogin API Credential pair. Obtained by creating API Credentials on
your OneLogin administration site. The credential must be given the "Manage All" scope/permission.
This value is considered write-only and cannot be obtained after being set.

IdentityProviderConfigOneLoginMfaPropert.description:
When the TypeReferenceName property is "OneLoginMfa", this provides additional configuration
values specific to that provider type.

ServerAddress1.description: The IP address or FQDN of the primary RADIUS server. (required for radius providers).

ServerAddress2.description: The IP address or FQDN of the backup RADIUS server.

ServerPort.description: The port number to use for connections to the RADIUS servers.  (required for radius providers).

SharedSecret.description: The shared secret used to hash RADIUS user passwords. This value is typically provided by a RADIUS system administrator.  (required for radius providers).

Timeout.description: The time (specified in seconds) to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before retrying.

Retries.description: The number of times to retry RADIUS server requests before giving up.

PreAuthenticateForChallengeResponse.description:
Indicates whether an Access-Request call containing only the User-Name should be sent to the RADIUS server
            prior to the user's authentication attempt.  This is done to inform the RADIUS server of the user's identity so it
            can possibly begin the authentication process by starting a challenge/response cycle.  This may be required to seed
            the user's state data.  In addition, the RADIUS server's response may include a login message that is to be displayed,
            which is specific to that user.  Note, if the RADIUS server is not configured to respond with an Access-Challenge,
            then this will cause the log in to fail and the user will be unable to proceed.

AlwaysMaskUserInput.description:
If enabled, the text box that the user enters their one-time password, or other challenge required by the
            RADIUS server, will always be a password style text box in which the user's input is masked, i.e. appears as a series
            of dots, not as clear text.  This may be desired when the challenge is not just a one-time password, but also contains
            the user's PIN.  This will prevent any passer-by from seeing the private information.  Note, however, that when this
            setting is enabled, it will also override the Prompt attribute of the RADIUS server's Access-Challenge response, such
            that the user's input will always be masked.

IdentityProviderConfigRadiusProperties.description:
When the TypeReferenceName property is "Radius", this provides additional RADIUS-specific
configuration values.

ForestRootDomain.description: The name of the forest root domain (Read-only).

SynchronizationIntervalMinutes.description: How frequently data imported from this directory should be synchronized with changes made in AD (in minutes).

LastFailureDeleteSyncDate.description: The time this directory last failed to synchronize deletions (Read-only).

FailedDeleteSyncAttempts.description: Number of subsequent failed delete sync attempts since last success (Read-only).

Domains.description: List of available domains that can be search from IdentityProviders representing a directory (Read-only).

IdentityProviderDirectoryProperties.description: Configuration settings for when the TypeReferenceName property is 'ActiveDirectory' or 'Ldap'.

DirectoryId.description: Use service account credentials from specified directory provider.

NetworkAddress.description: The network address of the server the directory resides on (not required for Active Directory).

DomainControllers.description: List of available domain controllers. If this list is empty then DNS will be used instead.

IdentityProviderSchemaParameters.description: Credential parameters for directory schema discovery.

HasToken.description: Whether there is a secret token assigned to the SCIM service. (Read-only).

TokenCreationDate.description: Date the secret token was created (Read-only).

IdentityProviderScimProperties.description: Represents properties related to SCIM identity providers.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderId.description: The primary authentication provider to be assigned to new SCIM users.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The friendly display name of the specified primary authentication provider.  (Read-only).

RequireCertificateAuthentication.description:
If set to `true`, then the user must authenticate with a client certificate. If they attempt to login
            with a username and password, for example, if they are using their Active Directory account, access will be denied.
            Cannot be disabled if enforced by Active Directory.  This is only applicable when Active Directory is being used
            to authenticate a user.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderId.description:
Specify the secondary authentication provider that all members will use when authenticating against
            Safeguard.  Note, this will only be applied to new users being added to Safeguard at the time the user is provisioned.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The friendly display name of the specified secondary authentication provider.  (Read-only).

AllowPersonalAccounts.description:
When set to `true`, will let the directory user create personal accounts
            in Safeguard.

AdminRoles.description:
Administrative roles that each provisioned user will be granted. e.g. GlobalAdmin, DirectoryAdmin,
            Auditor, AssetAdmin, ApplianceAdmin, PolicyAdmin, UserAdmin, HelpdeskAdmin, OperationsAdmin.

IdentityProviderScimUserTemplate.description: User property defaults to be used when a new SCIM user is provisioned outside of a UserGroup.

HasApiKey.description: Flag indicating whether there is an API key associated with this identity provider (Read-only).

Name.description: The name of the identity provider type. (Read-only).

AllowPrimaryAuthFlag.description: A flag indicating whether or not this identity provider type supports primary authentication. (Read-only).

AllowSecondaryAuthFlag.description: A flag indicating whether or not this identity provider type supports secondary authentication. (Read-only).

AllowMultipleConfigsFlag.description: A flag indicating whether or not multiple identity providers of this type may be configured. (Read-only).

DefaultServerPort.description: When given, provides the default server port number for the identity provider configuration. (Read-only).

IdentityProviderType.description: Represents the read-only property values of a supported identity provider.

SessionAccessAccountName.description: Name of session access account if using user-supplied credentials.

UseSra.description: Indicates whether the session is intended to be launch in the browser.

AutoAcceptSshHostKey.description: Whether to auto-accept the ssh host key.

CustomScriptParameters.description: Set of parameters to pass to custom platform script.

The date and time that the license expires, or null

The number of days remaining until the license expires, or
null if the license is perpetual. Will be negative if the
license is already expired.

For a trial license, the date by which it must be installed.
If no start-by date is present, this will return null.

TrialDays.description:
For a trial license, the number of days following installation that
the license remains valid. Null if not present.

has expired.

The date that this license expires, or null
if it is perpetual.

Id.description: The user's unique id.  This is a system-assigned value that is generated when a user is first created. (Read-only).

Name.description: Friendly identifier of the user's identity in Safeguard.  Must be unique per identity provider.

DomainName.description: Name of the domain this user is from.

Disabled.description: A flag indicating whether or not the user is disabled.

Locked.description: A flag indicating that the user's account is locked.  A locked user account may be unlocked by an administrator.

Base64PhotoData.description: Binary photo data. Base64 encoded.  Limited to 64 KB when decoded.  Must be a valid BMP, GIF, JPEG, PNG, or TIFF image.

LastLoginDate.description: Date and Time when the user last successfully logged in.

Reason.description: The reason the user is counted toward the license, if applicable.

Policies.description: The policies the user is assigned to, if applicable.

LicenseUsageUser.description: Information about a user that is potentially counted toward a license.

OtherUsers.description: List of users that do not count toward the license.

LicenseUserUsage.description: Information about how objects are counted toward a license.

ListOperation.description: Type of operation to perform on a list.

LogId.description: The unique id of this log entry.

UserId.description: Unique id of the user that authenticated.

ApplianceId.description: Id of appliance to which user authenticated.

ApplianceName.description: Name of appliance to which user authenticated.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderId.description: The id of the user's primary authentication provider.

PrimaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The name of the user's primary authentication provider.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderId.description: The id of the user's secondary authentication provider.  If this property is not set then the user is not subject to secondary authentication.

SecondaryAuthenticationProviderName.description: The name of the user's secondary authentication provider. (Read-only).

UpdatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that updated this entity (Read-only).

UpdatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that updated this entity (Read-only).

UserToken.description:
Upon successful authorization, a new access token will be granted that is to be used for all future API
            requests by the client application.  Currently, the lifetime of this token is one day.

PrimaryProviderId.description: If the access token from the STS represents a user that requires two-factor authentication, then the client application will have to redirect the user back to the STS, requesting that factor of authentication. This property will contain what directory service provider the user initially authenticated against. It will need to be included in the redirect back to the STS.

SecondaryProviderId.description: If the access token from the STS represents a user that requires two-factor authentication, then the client application will have to redirect the user back to the STS, requesting that factor of authentication. This property will contain what directory service provider the user needs for two-factor authentication. It will need to be included in the redirect back to the STS.

WebClientInactivityTimeout.description: This is the amount of time in minutes the web client can sit idle before its session will time out.

DesktopClientInactivityTimeout.description: (Deprecated) This is the amount of time in minutes the desktop client can sit idle before its session will time out.

LoginResponse.description: Used by client applications to exchange an STS token for a Safeguard user token that will then be used by the application for all future API requests. A client application should make a request to the /service/core/v2/Token/LoginResponse end point, passing the access token they got from the STS. Authorization will be performed on the authenticated user and if successful, this Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.LoginResponse object will be returned with a new token value in Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.LoginResponse.UserToken.

StsAccessToken.description: The access token obtained from the STS that the client application intends to exchange for a Safeguard token.

LoginResponseRequestData.description: When a client application makes a request to the /service/core/v2/Token/LoginResponse end point, they should post this object. Currently, we have only one property, so it could be a single parameter, but this will allow for future updates without necessarily breaking the clients.

LoginResponseStatus.description: The /service/core/v2/Token/LoginResponse end point will return a Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.LoginResponse object with a Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Users.LoginResponse.Status of one of these values indicating to the client application what it should do next.

Id.description: Unique database ID of this entity.

IsSystemOwned.description: Whether this is a system owned entity. If true, entity cannot be modified or deleted.

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

Subnets.description:
Set of subnets managed by this entity. A subnet may not be assigned
to multiple managed networks though overlapping subnets are allowed.

Members.description: Set of network members that are assigned to managed this network.

ManagedNetwork.description:
Represents a set of network subnets that are used to associate assets with appliances that will
provide services to those assets e.g., password check/change and access request sessions. This association
is automatically assigned based on IP address but can be manually overridden by the user.

Name.description: Friendly identifier of the user's identity in Safeguard.  Must be unique per identity provider.

Fido2Authenticators.description: The current user's FIDO2 authenticators that they have registered (Read-Only).

AdminRoles.description: Administrative roles this user has been granted. e.g. GlobalAdmin, Auditor, ApplicationAuditor, SystemAuditor, AssetAdmin, ApplianceAdmin, PolicyAdmin, UserAdmin, HelpdeskAdmin, OperationsAdmin.

Id.description: The user's unique id.  This is a system-assigned value that is generated when a user is first created. (Read-only).

LastName.description: The user's last name. No double quotes.

FirstName.description: The user's first name. No double quotes.

Disabled.description:
A flag indicating whether or not the user is disabled. Read-only for directory users
for which this will be synchronized with the directory.

TimeZoneId.description: The id of the user's timezone.

TimeZoneDisplayName.description: The description of the user's timezone (Read-only).

TimeZoneIanaName.description: The IANA name of the user's timezone (Read-only).

IsPartitionOwner.description: Whether this user owns any partitions (Read-only).

AllowPersonalAccounts.description: If the user is able to create the personal accounts.

Locked.description: A read-only flag indicating that the user's account is locked.  A locked user account may be unlocked by an administrator. (Read-only).

PasswordNeverExpires.description: A flag indicating that the user's password never expires. Only meaningful for users whose PrimaryAuthenticationType is 'Local'.

ChangePasswordAtNextLogin.description: If set to `true`, then the user must change their password at their next login.  Only meaningful for users whose PrimaryAuthenticationType is 'Local'.

Base64PhotoData.description: Binary photo data. Base64 encoded.  Limited to 64 KB when decoded.  Must be a valid BMP, GIF, JPEG, PNG, or TIFF image.

IsSystemOwned.description: Whether this is a built-in user that cannot be modified (Read-only).

IsRequester.description: Whether this user has any requester roles (Read-only).

IsApprover.description: Whether this user has any approver roles (Read-only).

IsReviewer.description: Whether this user has any reviewer roles (Read-only).

LastLoginDate.description: Date and Time when the user last successfully logged in (Read-only).

LastRequestDate.description: Date and Time when the user last submitted a request (Read-only).

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

ModifiedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that modified this entity (Read-only).

RequireCertificateAuthentication.description:
If set to `true`, then the user must authenticate with a client certificate.  If they attempt to login with a username and
password, for example, if they are using their Active Directory account, access will be denied. Cannot be disabled if enforced
by Active Directory.

DirectoryRequireCertificateAuthenticatio.description:
If this is an AD user then this will represent the 'Require Smart Card' attribute of the user in AD. If this is true then
RequireCertificateAuthentication will also be set to true.

LinkedAccountsCount.description: The number of policy accounts linked to the user (Read-only).

Id.description: The id of the user's primary authentication provider.

Name.description: The name of the user's primary authentication provider (Read-only).

Identity.description:
The value required to authenticate the user against the specified authentication provider.  Often this will be
what the user enters when logging in.  However, for some providers such as External Federation, it needs to match
the provider's Name Claim that is returned.  This value must be unique per authentication provider.  For Active
Directory and LDAP, you may specify the 'samAccountName' or 'Username' attribute when creating a user, but the
value will be overwritten with the 'objectGUID' or 'entryUuid', respectively.

Time allowed, in seconds, for network scan to run before cancelling the task. Set to 0 to use the
default of 5 days. Network scans over large ranges of IP addresses, especially when a machine is not
listening or cannot be identified, can take a long time.
