Id.description: Unique ID of the sync group (Read-Only).

ProfileId.description: Unique ID of the profile this sync group belongs to.

ProfileName.description: Name of the profile this sync group belongs to.

Disabled.description: Whether platform tasks can run against this sync group.

AssetPartitionId.description: Unique ID of the asset partition this sync group belongs to.

AssetPartitionName.description: Name of the partition this sync group belongs to.

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

LastSyncAccountsDate.description: The date/time of the last password change (Read-only).

LastSuccessSyncAccountsDate.description: The date/time of the last successful password change (Read-only).

LastFailureSyncAccountsDate.description: The date/time of the last failed password change (Read-only).

LastSyncAccountsTaskId.description: The task audit log ID of the last password change (Read-only).

FailedSyncAccountsAttempts.description: Number of subsequent failed password change attempts since last success (Read-only).

NextSyncAccountsDate.description: The date/time of the next password change, if this account is enabled for automatic password management (Read-only).

IsSynchronized.description: Whether all of the accounts for this sync group are currently synchronized.

Accounts.description: Set of accounts that are assigned to this sync group.

PasswordSyncGroup.description:
Represents a group of accounts in a profile whose passwords must be the same. All accounts will be updated in priority order
when a password change is requested.

ApplianceId.description: The Appliance Id of the appliance the patch precondition check was run on.

IsOsPatch.description:
True if this is an Operating System Patch, in which case use Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.PatchPreconditionResult.TargetOsLevel.
False if this a Safeguard Patch, in which case use Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.PatchPreconditionResult.PatchVersion.
Null if the patch metadata is unavailable.

TargetOsLevel.description: Contains the Operating System target level if Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V3.Patch.PatchPreconditionResult.IsOsPatch is true.

Errors.description: Errors which were detected when running the patch precondition check.

Warnings.description: Warnings which were detected when running the patch precondition check.

PatchVersion.description: Specific to patching, will get translated to appliance Version after install.

Id.description: The personal account's unique id.  This is a system-assigned value that is generated when an account is first created. (Read-only).

Name.description: The descriptive name for the personal account.

AccountName.description: The personal account name.

Url.description: The URL that is associated with the personal account.

Notes.description: The notes that are associated with the password account.

ExpirationDate.description: Expiration date of the personal account password.

LastChangeDate.description: The date that the personal password was changed (Read-only).

DisableNotifications.description: A flag indicating whether or not expiration notifications should be sent to the owner of the personal account.

OwnerId.description: Owner Id of the personal account. (Read-Only).

OwnerDisplayName.description: The display name of the owner of the personal account. (Read-only).

OwnerEmailAddress.description: The email address of the owner of the personal account. (Read-only).

OwnerDomainName.description: The domain name of the owner of the personal account. (Read-only).

SharedWithId.description: Id of the user with whom the personal account is shared.

ShareExpirationDate.description: Expiration date of the personal account share.

IsOwnedByMe.description: A flag indicating whether the personal account is owned by the current user.

IsSharedByMe.description: A flag indicating whether the personal account is currently being shared.

IsShareable.description: A flag indicating whether the personal account is currently shareable.

OwnerId.description: Owner Id of the personal account. (Read-Only).

TimeStarted.description: Time validity start for personal account password. (Read-Only).

TimeEnded.description: Time validity end for personal account password. (Read-Only).

PersonalAccountPasswordRule.description: Provides parameters for personal account password generation.

SharedWithId.description: The Id of the user with whom the personal account is shared.

ShareExpirationDate.description: Expiration date of the personal account share.

PersonalAccountShare.description: Represents a shared personal password account.

Id.description: The database ID of the platform version (Read-only).

IsAcctNameCaseSensitive.description: Does the platform require case sensitive account names?.

IsSystemOwned.description: If system owned then this platform cannot be modified or deleted (Read-only).

Platform.description: Represents a set of platform specific properties for an asset.

DefaultPort.description: The default port to use for connections (Read-only).

DefaultSslPort.description: The default port to use for connections when UseSslEncryption is enabled (Read-only).

AllowCustomPort.description:
Can the end user specify a port other than the default?
Valid for all but Windows, Windows Active Directory, Windows Desktop, and SAP (Read-only).

SupportsTimeout.description: Whether platform supports standard connection timeout parameter (Read-only).

SupportsPasswordAuthentication.description: Whether a service account can login using a password on this platform (Read-only).

SupportsSshKeyAuthentication.description: Whether a service account can login using an SSH key (Read-only).

SupportsLocalhostPasswordAuthentication.description: Whether a service account can login using an account on the hosted server with a password (Read-only).

SupportsDirectoryPasswordAuthentication.description: Whether a service account can login using an account from a directory server with a password (Read-only).

SupportsAccessKeyAuthentication.description: Whether this platform requires access key credentials.

SupportsAccountPasswordAuthentication.description: Whether this platform requires the current account password for check/change.

SupportsStarlingConnectAuthentication.description: Whether this platform can use the starling join credential for authentication.

SupportsApiKeyAuthentication.description: Whether this platform supports an API Key authentication.

SupportsPrivilegeElevationCommand.description:
Whether or not the platform supports delegation of commands (e.g. sudo.) The command is used to preface
commands requiring privileged access on the asset. Note that this must be non-interactive.
Valid for most unix-compatible platforms (Read-only).

SupportsHideAlterUserCommand.description: Whether the platform supports using a transaction to not log the Alter User commands in the database system logs. (PostgreSQL only).

SupportsSslEncryption.description: Whether the platform supports encryption of telnet data.

SupportsWorkstationId.description:
Whether the platform supports assigning a workstation ID to the telnet session
Valid for AS/400 platforms (Read-only).

SupportsSshTransport.description: Whether this platform uses SSH to connect to the target machine.

SupportsEnablePassword.description: Cisco routers support an extra password to enable password changes.

SupportsInstance.description: Whether this ODBC platform supports an Instance name to identify the target.

SupportsServiceName.description: Whether this ODBC platform supports a Service name to identify the target.

SupportsSslCertificateVerification.description: Whether this platform supports verification of the SSL certificate.

SupportsSslThumbprint.description: Whether this platform uses a nominated SSL CA certificate.

SupportsClientId.description:
Whether the platform requires a client ID to be specified
Valid for SAP platforms (Read-only).

RequiresDistinguishedName.description:
Whether a distinguished name is required for accounts
Valid for LDAP-based platforms (Read-only).

SupportsOraclePrivileges.description: Whether this platform supports 'as privileges' property (Oracle only).

SupportsTacacsSecret.description: Whether this platform supports Tacacs Secret (Cisco ISE only).

SupportsLdap.description: Set to true if this platform is a directory that supports LDAP (read-only).

SupportsDirectorySync.description: Set to true if this platform is a directory that supports directory synchronization (read-only).

SupportsDirectoryAssetDiscovery.description: Set to true if this platform is a directory that supports asset discovery (read-only).

SupportsLocalAssetDiscovery.description: Set to true if this platform supports asset discovery (read-only).

SupportsNamedPipeForServiceAccountConnec.description:
Whether named pipes or TcpIp is used for service account connection to the platform
Valid for Sql Server platforms.

SupportsTopSecretInterval.description: Whether to use the profile's next change date as the interval on a Top Secret mainframe.

SupportsPrivilegedAccount.description:
Whether this platform supports a privileged account that can be managed, but cannot be used as a service account
Valid for Checkpoint platforms.

SupportsHttpProxy.description: Whether this platform supports HTTP proxy settings.

PrivilegedAccountName.description:
The name of the privileged account, if supported
Valid for Checkpoint platforms.

PlatformConnectionProperties.description: Default session feature settings for this platform.

HasScript.description: Whether a script has been uploaded for this custom platform (Read-only).

Parameters.description: A list of parameters that should be provided to the custom platform script when invoked.

PlatformCustomScriptProperties.description: Settings related to session access requests.

SupportsPasswordManagement.description: Whether this platform supports automated management of account passwords.

SupportsSshKeyManagement.description: Whether this platform supports automated management of account SSH keys.

SupportsApiKeyManagement.description: Whether this platform supports automated management of API keys.

SupportsFileManagement.description: Whether this platform supports automated management of files.

SupportsAccountDiscovery.description: Whether this platform supports account discovery.

SupportsDependentSystemTasks.description: Whether this platform allows local service/task updates of directory account passwords.

SupportsCustomDependencyUpdate.description: Whether the platform supports custom updates of dependent local/domain accounts.

AllowRequireCurrentPassword.description: Whether this platform supports use of the current account password when managing passwords.

RequireNetworkAddress.description: Whether a network address is required for this platform.

SupportsServiceDiscovery.description: Whether this platform supports service discovery.

SupportsUsePasswordHash.description: Set to true if this platform is a directory that supports password hashing management itself (read-only).

SupportsSuspendRestoreAccount.description: Whether this platform allows accounts to be suspended/restored (disabled/enabled).

SupportsElevateDemoteAccount.description: Whether this platform allows accounts to be elevated/demoted.

SupportsTotp.description: Whether this platform supports TOTP Credentials.

RequireApiKeySecretId.description: Whether this platform requires an API key secret ID.

SupportsApiKeyExpiration.description: Whether this platform supports an API key expiration.

SupportsApiKeyApplicationAccounts.description: Whether this platform supports API key accounts.

PlatformPasswordFeatureProperties.description: Settings related to password management features.

Id.description: ID of the task (Read-only).

PlatformId.description: Unique ID of the platform the script is associated with.

PlatformDisplayName.description: Display name of the platform the script is associated with.

LogTime.description: The date the script was modified.

PlatformScriptLog.description: Represents metadata for platform script changes.

SupportsSessionManagement.description: Whether this platform supports session access requests.

DefaultRemoteDesktopSessionPort.description: Default port for Remote Desktop sessions.

PlatformSessionFeatureProperties.description: Settings related to session access requests.

LastTaskDate.description: The date/time of the last attempt (Read-only).

LastSuccessTaskDate.description: The date/time of the last successful attempt (Read-only).

LastFailureTaskDate.description: The date/time of the last failed attempt (Read-only).

LastTaskId.description: The task audit log ID of the last attempt (Read-only).

FailedTaskAttempts.description: Number of subsequent failed attempts since last success (Read-only).

NextTaskDate.description: The date/time of the next attempt, if this account is enabled for automatic password management (Read-only).

PlatformTaskInformation.description: Information about the platform task execution (Read-only).

Id.description: The database key of the account (Read-only).

Description.description: A short description of the account. (Read-only).

HasPassword.description: Whether the account has a password set or not (Read-only).

HasSshKey.description: Whether the account has an SSH key assigned or not (Read-only).

HasTotpAuthenticator.description: Whether the account has TOTP Authenticator or not (Read-only).

HasApiKeys.description: Whether the account has an API key assigned or not (Read-only).

HasFile.description: Whether the account has a file assigned or not (Read-only).

DomainName.description: The domain name that the account belongs to. (Read-only).

DistinguishedName.description: The distinguished name of the account. (Read-only).

NetBiosName.description: The netbios name of the domain the account is from. (Read-only).

Disabled.description: Whether this account has been disabled. Prevent it from being used for access requests and automated password tasks (Read-only).

IsServiceAccount.description: Whether this account is being used as a service account (Read-only).

IsApplicationAccount.description: Whether this account represents an application for API keys (Read-only).

NotifyOwnersOnly.description: Only notify account owners that password must be changed or account must be restored/suspended rather than running immediately (Read-only).

SuspendAccountWhenCheckedIn.description: Whether an account should be suspended when it is checked in. Will be restored when made available for check out. (Read-only).

DemoteAccountWhenCheckedIn.description: Whether an account should be demoted when it is checked in. Will be elevated when made available for check out. (Read-only).

AltLoginName.description: The alternate login name of the account. (Read-only).

PrivilegeGroupMembership.description: The elevation group names of the account. (Read-only).

LinkedUsersCount.description: The number of users linked to the policy account (Read-only).

PolicyAccount.description:
Represents an remote asset account available for request. A PolicyAccount is an
alternate view of an account that is used for AccessPolicies, AccountGroups,
and UserFavorites. The account must have a password and have AllowPasswordRequest set to true
in order to be used in UserFavorites or to be able to request the password of the account.

RequireApproval.description: Password requests must be approved before the password may be released.

PendingApprovalEscalationEnabled.description: Whether email notifications should be sent for approval escalation.

PendingApprovalDurationBeforeEscalationD.description: The maximum duration (days) a access request may be pending approval before notifying escalation contacts.

PendingApprovalDurationBeforeEscalationH.description: The maximum duration (hours) a access request may be pending approval before notifying escalation contacts.

PendingApprovalDurationBeforeEscalationM.description: The maximum duration (minutes) a access request may be pending approval before notifying escalation contacts.

PolicyApproverProperties.description: Settings related to approving a access request.

Id.description: The database key of the asset (Read-only).

NetworkAddress.description: The network address of the asset (Read-only).

Description.description: A short description of the asset. (Read-only).

AssetPartitionId.description: Database ID of partition asset belongs to.

AssetPartitionName.description: Name of partition asset belongs to.

DomainName.description: The domain name that the asset belongs to. (Read-only).

Disabled.description: Whether this asset has been disabled. Prevent it from being used for access requests and automated password tasks (Read-only).

PolicyAsset.description:
Represents an remote asset available for request. A PolicyAsset is an
alternate view of an asset that is used for AccessPolicies, AssetGroups,
and UserFavorites. The asset must have a password and have AllowSessionRequests set to true
in order to be used in UserFavorites or to be able to request a session on the asset.

Id.description: The database ID of the type of platform (Read-only).

DisplayName.description: The display name of the type of platform (Read-only).

SupportsSessionManagement.description: Whether this asset supports session access.

AllowEmergencyAccess.description: Whether the members of this role can request emergency access.

IgnoreHourlyRestrictions.description: Ignore hourly restrictions when requesting emergency access.

PolicyEmergencyAccessProperties.description: Settings related to emergency access to a password.

DefaultReleaseDurationDays.description:
The default duration (days) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. DefaultReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

DefaultReleaseDurationHours.description:
The default duration (hours) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. DefaultReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

DefaultReleaseDurationMinutes.description:
The default duration (minutes) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. DefaultReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

MaximumReleaseDurationDays.description:
The maximum duration (days) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. MaximumReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

MaximumReleaseDurationHours.description:
The maximum duration (hours) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. MaximumReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

MaximumReleaseDurationMinutes.description:
The maximum duration (minutes) a request may be available. Only needed
when AllowCustomDuration is true. MaximumReleaseDuration must be less than 31 days.

AllowCustomDuration.description:
Whether to allow the release duration to be modified during a
access request.

RequireReasonCode.description: A reason code is required before a access request can be made.

RequireReasonComment.description: A reason comment is required before a access request can be made.

RequireServiceTicket.description: Whether a service ticket is required for access requests.

RequiredReviewers.description: The minimum number of reviews required before a password request is closed.

RequireReviewerComment.description: Whether a reviewer is required to supply a comment.

AllowSubsequentAccessRequestsWithoutRevi.description: Whether this policy will allow bypassing any pending reviews in order to create new requests.

PendingReviewEscalationEnabled.description: Whether email notifications should be sent for approval escalation.

PendingReviewDurationBeforeEscalationDay.description: The maximum duration (days) an expired password release may be pending review before notifying escalation contacts.

PendingReviewDurationBeforeEscalationHou.description: The maximum duration (hours) a expired password release may be pending review before notifying escalation contacts.

PendingReviewDurationBeforeEscalationMin.description: The maximum duration (minutes) a expired password release may be pending review before notifying escalation contacts.

PolicyReviewerProperties.description: Settings related to reviewing a password request.

Id.description: Database Id of the scope item.

PolicyScopeItem.description: Represents requestable items governed by policy.

Id.description: Id of the Role.

Priority.description:
The priority determines the processing order of the Role.
Roles will be processed in order which may cause some priorities to be shifted lower.

Expires.description:
The date and time that the license expires, or null
if the license is a perpetual license.

DaysRemaining.description:
The number of days remaining until the license expires, or
null if the license is perpetual. Will be negative if the
license is already expired.

InstallByDate.description:
For a trial license, the date by which it must be installed.
If no start-by date is present, this will return null.

TrialDays.description:
For a trial license, the number of days following installation that
the license remains valid. Null if not present.

ChangedByUserDisplayName.description:
The name of the user that installed the license, or
null for a license that is not installed.

ChangedByUserId.description:
The id of the user that installed the license, or
null for a license that is not installed.

ChangedByDate.description:
The date and time that the license was installed, or
null if the license is not installed.

ProductVersion.description: Product version of the code/image of the appliance.

DisplayName.description: Display name of the property that changed.

Type.description: The type name of the property that changed.

OldValue.description: The old value of the property that changed.

NewValue.description: The new value of the property that changed.

PropertyChange.description: Represents a change made to an object.

AutoPurgeAssets.description: Whether to automatically purged expired deleted assets.

DeletedAssetRetentionInDays.description: How long to retain deleted assets before purging.

AutoPurgeAssetAccounts.description: Whether to automatically purged expired deleted asset accounts.

DeletedAssetAccountRetentionInDays.description: How long to retain deleted asset accounts before purging.

AutoPurgeUsers.description: Whether to automatically purged expired deleted users.

DeletedUserRetentionInDays.description: How long to retain deleted using before purging.

PurgeSettings.description: Represents setting governing how long to retain deleted entities.

Id.description: The database ID of the reason code (Read-only).

Name.description: The name of the reason code.

ReasonCode.description: Represents the reason when requesting the password of an account.

Id.description: The registration's unique id.  This is a system-assigned value that is generated when a registration is first created. (Read-only).

Disabled.description: A flag indicating whether or not the application registration is disabled. Will be disabled if certificate user is disabled.

VisibleToCertificateUsers.description: A flag indicating whether or not the application self discovery is enabled for the cert user.

BidirectionalEnabled.description: A flag indicating whether or not the A2A registration supports credentials changes.

CertificateUserId.description: The id of the certificate user associated with the registration.

CertificateUserThumbPrint.description: Thumbprint associated with the certificate user.

CertificateUser.description: The accounts for which the credentials can be retrieved.

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

Registration.description: Represents an application to application registration.

Users.description: The users for which an access request can be created.

Groups.description: The user group for which an access request can be created.

RegistrationAccessRequestBroker.description: Represents the required details to create an access request on behalf of another user.

UserId.description: User ID.

Disabled.description: User disabled flag.

DomainName.description: Domain name.

RegistrationAlias.description: Represents a user that an application can impersonate.

DomainName.description: Domain name.

RegistrationAliasGroup.description: Represents a user group whose users an application can impersonate.

AccountId.description: The account's unique id.

AccountName.description: The account name.

AssetId.description: ID of the asset.

AssetName.description: Name of the asset.

AssetPartitionId.description: ID of the asset partition.

AssetPartitionName.description: Name of the asset partition.

NetworkAddress.description: Network address of the asset.

DomainName.description: Domain name.

RegistrationRetrievableAccount.description: Represents an associated account on an application to application registration.

AuthenticationString.description: Authentication string used to connect to the api.

ApplicationHostAssetId.description: Database ID for the application host asset.

ApplicationHostAccountId.description: Database ID for the application host asset login account.

ApplicationDisplayName.description: Remote application display name (Windows Server and Desktop).

ApplicationProgram.description: Remote application program (Windows Server or Desktop) (path to OI-SG-RemoteApp-Launcher.exe).

ApplicationCmdLine.description: Remote application command line (Windows Server or Desktop) (Parameters to OI-SG-RemoteApp-Launcher: --cmd executable --args args_to_executable).

ApplicationHostUserSupplied.description: Are the credentials user supplied for the application host.

RemoteDesktopApplicationProperties.description: Configuration specific to Remote Desktop application sessions.

PercentComplete.description: How much of work associated with the request is complete (Read-only).

Cancellable.description: Is the request cancellable? (Read-only). The value of this flag is a suggestion only.

AcceptanceDuration.description: Duration that request was being validated for acceptance (Read-only).

StartTime.description: When was the request work started (Read-only).

TotalDuration.description: Total duration that request was active (Read-only).

Message.description: Last message associated with the request (Read-only).

RequestId.description: Unique ID of the access request to review.

Comment.description: Comment made by reviewer to describe review.

Id.description: The database ID of the role (Read-only).

Priority.description:
The priority of this role. Used for resolving permission conflicts.
Set to 0 to add as the lowest priority role. Max role priority plus 1.

ExpirationDate.description: Date/time after which the associated policy/role will cease to be active.

HasExpiredPolicies.description: Whether this role has policies that are expired.  (Read-only).

HasInvalidPolicies.description: Whether this role has policies that have invalid connection policies.  (Read-only).

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

UserCount.description: Number of users that are a member of this role (Read-only).

AccountCount.description: Number of accounts that have policies belonging to this role (Read-only).

AssetCount.description: Number of assets that have policies belonging to this role (Read-only).

PolicyCount.description: Number of policies belonging to this role (Read-only).

Members.description: Identities that are members of this role. Requesters for password policies assigned to this role.

Role.description:
Roles in Pangaea are made up of a members, security scopes, and permissions.
* Members consist of users or groups.
* Permissions determine access to resources on the appliance.
* Security scopes determine access to assets such as assets and accounts. This access to assets is managed via policies (i.e. groups of settings).

RoleBasedAccountDiscoveryRuleType.description: Type of role account discovery filter.

NameFilter.description: Filters accounts based on the name of the account. Names can be specified as a regular expression.

RoleFilter.description: Filter accounts based on the names of role the account belongs to. Names can be specified as a regular expression.

PermissionFilter.description: Filter accounts based on the names of permissions the account belongs to. Names can be specified as a regular expression.

ApplianceId.description: Appliance Id that task is running on.

ApplianceName.description: Appliance name that task is running on.

AssetDiscoveryJobName.description: Name of the asset discovery job (Read-only).

AssetPartitionId.description: The asset partition this job belongs to (Read-only).

AssetPartitionName.description: Name of the asset partition this job belongs to (Read-only).

AssetId.description: Database ID of the asset or directory to execute this task on.

AssetName.description: Name of the asset or directory name the task was executed against (Read-only).

AccountId.description: Account to run this task for.

AccountName.description: Name of the account this task is for (Read-only).

ProfileId.description: ID of the profile to run this task for.

ProfileName.description: Name of the profile this task is for (Read-only).

SyncGroupId.description: ID of the sync group to run this task for.

SyncGroupName.description: Name of the sync group this task is for (Read-only).

RunningTask.description: Represents an asynchronous task on the appliance that is running.

AssetId.description: Database ID of asset to request access to.

AccountId.description: Database ID of the account to request password for (or access account for sessions).

AccountName.description: Name of the account being requested.

AccountDomainName.description: Name of the domain of the account.

AccountAssetId.description: Database ID of the asset of the account.

AccountAssetName.description: Name of the asset of the account.

AssetName.description: Name of the asset to request access for.

IsEmergency.description: Whether emergency access is being requested.

ReasonCodeId.description: Database ID of the pre-defined reason code for why the password access is needed.

ReasonCodeName.description: Name of the pre-defined reason code for why the password access is needed.

ReasonComment.description: Requester's reason of why the password access is needed.

RequestedDurationDays.description: The number of days requested for password access. The sum of Requested Days/Hours/Minutes must not exceed 7 days.

RequestedDurationHours.description: The number of hours requested for password access. The sum of Requested Days/Hours/Minutes must not exceed 7 days.

RequestedDurationMinutes.description: The number of minutes requested for password access. The sum of Requested Days/Hours/Minutes must not exceed 7 days.

ActiveRequests.description: List of requests that are currently active.

SavedAccessRequest.description: A saved access request that can be used in a user request favorite.

Command.description: The command to run on the asset.

CommandLineArguments.description: The arguments to pass to the command on the command line. Arguments containing space must be quoted.

StdinArgumentsList.description: An ordered list of arguments that will be written to stdin when the command executes. Each argument will be written as a separate line.

StopOnFail.description: If true, the password or SSH key change will only be attempted if the custom dependency command reports success. Only valid for Prep.

ReportExitStatus.description: If false, the custom dependency command will ignore errors and always report success. Only valid for Post, Fail.

LogCommand.description: If true, Safeguard will log the command that will be executed.

LogCommandArguments.description: If true, Safeguard will log the command line arguments.

LogStdinArguments.description: If true, Safeguard will log the arguments written to stdin.

LogStdout.description: If true, Safeguard will log the output produced by the command.

ScheduleCustomDependency.description: A command to be run on an asset before or after a password or SSH key change for a dependent account.

EndMinute.description: Minute the interval ends. The end minute is not included in the window interval.

Iterations.description: Number of times to execute tasks during schedule window (derived using RepeatInterval for Hourly and Minute schedule types).

ScheduleInterval.description: Interval of time in which to execute tasks.

ScheduleType.description: Represents how often the specified schedule will run.

Id.description: The database ID of the account (Read-only).

DistinguishedName.description: The distinguished name of the account (required for ACF2-Ldap).

DomainName.description: Name of the domain this object is from. Required if this is an object to import from a directory.

Description.description: A short description of the account.

Disabled.description: Whether this account has been disabled. Prevent it from being used for access requests and automated password tasks (Read-only).

Id.description: Database ID of the search (Read-only).

CreatedByUserId.description: The database ID of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

CreatedByUserDisplayName.description: The display name of the user that created this entity (Read-only).

TimeZoneId.description: The timezone in which the schedule should run. Required when ScheduleType is not Never.

TimeZoneDisplayName.description: The description of the timezone in which the schedule should run (Read-only).

RepeatInterval.description:
How frequently to run. Units are based on ScheduleType.
e.g. every 2 days, every 3 months, every 4 weeks.

RepeatDayOfMonth.description: Which day of the month the schedule should run. Only valid when RepeatMonthlyScheduleType is DayOfMonth.

RepeatDaysOfWeek.description: Which days of the week the schedule should run. Only valid when ScheduleType is Weekly.

StartHour.description: The hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Hour' or 'Minute' schedule type).

StartMinute.description: The minute of the hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Minute' schedule type).

TimeOfDayIntervals.description: Configuration of intervals of time in a day where tasks can be executed.

StartDateOption.description: Get activity that occurred after this date. Defaults to 1 day before endDate. (Preferred over filter).

EndDateOption.description: Get activity that occurred before this date. Defaults to now. (Preferred over filter).

UserIdOption.description: Get activity that occurred for a specific user (Preferred over filter).

UserIdOptionDisplayName.description: Display name for user ID option.

AssetIdOption.description: Get activity that occurred for a specific asset (Preferred over filter).

AssetIdOptionName.description: Display name for asset ID option.

AccountIdOption.description: Get activity that occurred for a specific account (Preferred over filter).

AccountIdOptionName.description: Display name for account ID option.

IncludeDetailsOption.description: Report all activity details as opposed to a summary view. Only valid when CategoryOption is specified.

FilterOption.description: Filter results. Only V3 DTO properties are supported. Available operators: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, and, or, not, contains, ieq, icontains, in [ {item1}, {item2}, etc], ().

PageOption.description: Which page (starting with 0) of data to return.

LimitOption.description: The size of a page of data.

CountOption.description:
When specified, the method will return a single integer value representing the total number of items that match the given filter criteria.
If specified, the fields and orderby parameter values are ignored.

FieldsOption.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to include in entity output. Only V3 DTO properties are supported.

OrderbyOption.description: List of property names (comma-separated) to sort entities by. Only V3 DTO properties are supported. Prepend properties with - for descending.

QueryStringOption.description: Search all string fields for the specified value.

LastScheduledExecution.description: The last time this report was executed.

NextScheduledExecution.description: The next time this report will be executed.

RdpShowWallpaper.description:
When enabled, any Remote Desktop (RDP) session request will include a client setting allowing for desktop wallpaper to be displayed.
Note, this only effects the client side RDP setting. The server may have a Group Policy Object (GPO) that overrides the client setting.
Also, this policy property overrides the setting in Safeguard Remote Access (SRA) for enabling wallpaper for RDP sessions.
Other things may also override the result too, depending on how the RDP connection information is used and made.

SpsApplianceId.description: ID of the SPS appliance server where the session recording resides.

IsLive.description: Is the session recording currently live.

MinValue.description: The setting's minimum limit value, if the value is numeric in nature. When null, the value is either non-numeric or there is no minimum limit. (Read-only).

MaxValue.description: The setting's maximum limit value, if the setting is numeric in nature. When null, the value is either non-numeric or there is no maximum limit. (Read-only).

Options.description: Valid options for this setting. Empty for many settings. (Read-only).

Id.description: The user's unique ID.  This is a system-assigned value that is generated when a user is first created. (Read-only).

Name.description: Friendly identifier of the user's identity in Safeguard.  Must be unique per identity provider.

LastName.description: The user's last name. No double quotes.

FirstName.description: The user's first name. No double quotes.

Base64PhotoData.description: Binary photo data. Base64 encoded.  Limited to 64 KB when decoded.  Must be a valid BMP, GIF, JPEG, PNG, or TIFF image.

SshAlgorithms.description: Represents the available algorithms when establishing an SSH connection through the sessions module, or to an archive server. The list order determines the priority in which the algorithms are negotiated with the SSHD server.

SuspendAccountWhenCheckedIn.description: Whether an account should be suspended when it is checked in. Will be restored when made available for check out.

NotifyOwnersOnly.description: Only notify account owners that password must be changed or account must be restored/suspended rather than running immediately.

e.g. every 2 days, every 3 months, every 4 weeks.

RepeatDaysOfWeek.description: Which days of the week the schedule should run. Only valid when ScheduleType is Weekly.

StartHour.description: The hour of the day that the schedule will run (not applicable for 'Hour' or 'Minute' schedule type).

Passphrase.description:
If the policy is set to password protect the SSH key, then this will contain the random
            passphrase that was used.  The passphrase, and therefore the SSH key data, will change each time
            the SSH key is downloaded/checked out.  May be null if the policy does not require the SSH key to
            be password protected. (Read-only).

SshKeySyncGroup.description:
Represents a group of accounts in a profile whose SSH keys must be the same. All accounts will be updated in priority order
when a SSH key change is requested.

SyncPriority.description:
This account will be synchronized at the same time as other accounts in the sync group with the same priority.
Accounts with lower priority will be synchronized first.

If set to `true`, then the user must authenticate with a client certificate.  If they attempt to login with a username and
password, for example, if they are using their Active Directory account, access will be denied. Cannot be disabled if enforced
by Active Directory.

RequireCertificateAuthentication will also be set to true.

AllowSessionPasswordRelease.description: Whether to allow the password to be released as part of a session access requests. Deprecated: Use IncludePasswordRelease instead.

AllowSessionSshKeyRelease.description: Whether to allow the SSH key to be released as part of a session access request (Ssh). Deprecated: Use IncludeSshKeyRelease instead.

IncludePasswordRelease.description:
For use with session access request policies and access request policies of type Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.File.
When set to `true`, if the account for which the access request also contains a password, then that password will also be included as part of the access request.
E.g. making it immediately available for checkout along with a file request. This can be useful in cases where the file may be an encrypted Zip file, for example. That way, the user
doesn't have to make a separate access request just for the password.

If the account does not allow explicit Password Requests, but does allow File Requests, and this property is set to `true`, then the password will still be released.

IncludeSshKeyRelease.description:
For use with access request policies of type Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.File and Pangaea.Data.Transfer.V2.Policies.AccessRequestType.Ssh/>.
When set to `true`, if the account for which the access request also contains an SSH key, then that SSH key will also be included as part of the access request.
E.g. making it immediately available for checkout along with the file. That way, the user doesn't have to make a separate access request just for the SSH key.

If the account does not allow explicit SSH Key Requests, but does allow File Requests, and this property is set to `true`, then the SSH key will still be released.

PassphraseProtectSshKey.description: Whether the SSH key will be protected with a passphrase each time it is checked out.

Identity.description:
The value required to authenticate the user against the specified authentication provider.  Often this will be
what the user enters when logging in.  However, for some providers such as External Federation, it needs to match
the provider's Name Claim that is returned.  This value must be unique per authentication provider.  For Active
Directory and LDAP, you may specify the 'samAccountName' or 'Username' attribute when creating a user, but the
value will be overwritten with the 'objectGUID' or 'entryUuid', respectively.

Name of attribute used while adding users of a directory user group from which the imported user's primary
            authentication identity (email address or name claim) will come from when an external federation provider has been
            chosen as that group's means of authentication.

DirectoryGroupSyncAttributeForRadiusAuth.description:
Name of attribute used while adding users of a directory user group from which the imported user's primary
            or secondary authentication identity will come from when a RADIUS provider has been chosen as that group's means of
            primary or secondary authentication.

DirectoryGroupSyncAttributeForManagedObj.description:
Name of attribute used while adding users of a directory user group from which the imported user's linked
            accounts will automatically be associated to their Safeguard user.
